pipeline: segment (U-Net, from scratch, binary plant/background) -> mask raw image -> classify (ResNet50, trained on ground-truth-masked images)
status: RECOMMENDED PIPELINE. Outperforms both the DeepLabV3 variant (export_leaf_pipeline/, 84.4%) and an earlier version of this same U-Net pipeline (78.75%) that used U-Net's own native direct-resize preprocessing. This version instead feeds U-Net the SAME Resize(256)->CenterCrop(224) image the classifier expects, which fixed a mask/classifier geometry misalignment -- segmentation quality itself barely changed between the two U-Net variants (see below), so the earlier version's weaker accuracy was almost entirely down to that mismatch, not U-Net's segmentation ability.

segmentation_model.architecture: U-Net (Ronneberger et al.), trained from scratch, no pretrained backbone
segmentation_model.output: binary segmentation mask, 1 channel logits, sigmoid + threshold 0.5 applied at inference
segmentation_model.preprocessing: Resize(256)->CenterCrop(224) (aspect-preserving), ImageNet mean/std normalize -- NOTE: this is NOT U-Net's own original training/eval convention (which used a direct resize distorting aspect ratio); it's deliberately changed here to match the classifier's expected geometry, which measurably improved full-pipeline accuracy (see below).

classifier_model.trained_on: tomato final dataset_masked (ground-truth-COCO-polygon-masked images, Resize(256)->CenterCrop(224) preprocessing)
classifier_model.note: IDENTICAL weights to export_leaf_pipeline/resnet50_leaf_classifier.pth -- same classifier reused across all segmenter variants for a fair comparison.

preprocessing_fix_history: An earlier version of this export used U-Net's own native direct-resize-to-224 preprocessing (distorts aspect ratio), matching how U-Net was originally trained. That version scored only 78.75% full-pipeline accuracy despite reasonable segmentation quality, because the resulting mask didn't align with the Resize+CenterCrop geometry the classifier expects. Switching U-Net's inference-time preprocessing to Resize(256)->CenterCrop(224) (tested empirically, not assumed) raised full-pipeline accuracy to 89.4% while segmentation IoU/Dice stayed essentially flat -- confirming the earlier bottleneck was geometry alignment, not segmentation quality.

comparison_table.test_set: tomato final dataset/test (160 images, all with matching COCO polygon annotations, 0 skipped)

full_pipeline_test_accuracy.note: crop-aligned U-Net is now the best of all three

flowering.note: the class most affected by the geometry fix -- recall went from 0.52 (native) to 0.85 (crop-aligned)

fruiting.note: weakest class across ALL variants -- smallest class, occluded by fruit clusters, a data property not fixed by preprocessing

source_files.evaluation_notebook: unet_leaf_pipeline_comparison.ipynb (this project) -- see the 'crop-aligned' variant section
